authority: repository-local runbook, documentation, policy, test, Git commit, main push, and remote-ref readback only; no provider, workflow, deployment, credential, message, approval-resume, release, or production operation

checks.result: Two app indexes route exactly ten canonical runbooks. check:docs passed 219 files; all 43 tooling tests reject missing inventory or sections, embedded secrets, mutable provider actuality, and tool-derived authority.
Root, app, package, SPEC, plan, audit, docs-maintainer, and research routes resolve to the app owners; check:skills passed 167 files and 16 mirrors.
A fresh Terra evaluator selected the correct owner and safe stop in all six deployment, Executor, Sendblue, proof-boundary, reauthentication, and unsupported-revocation scenarios.

Effect setup, boundaries, docs and skills, 43 tooling tests, Ultracite, lint, Knip, five typechecks, and 250 package/app tests passed with schema-valid synthetic Executor configuration and no provider call.
Semantic commit 9803c307d5ed8b1ad097270b34fb55ef5876b2bb was pushed; after fetch, local HEAD and origin/main both resolved to that commit with tree c7218be4f4c1f10b50ffd696f53114ff056a6452.

postcondition: Bundjil now routes local development, deployment, Executor, Sendblue, proxy authentication, proof, reauthentication, incident, rollback, and revocation work through ten target-owned procedures with explicit authority, readback, stop, rollback, escalation, limitations, and non-claims.
rollback: Revert the HGI-303 closeout and 9803c307d5ed8b1ad097270b34fb55ef5876b2bb together while preserving HGI-300 through HGI-302, HGI-308, and HGI-310 evidence and all implementation source.
limitations: Runbook, source, local-check, and fresh-context success do not prove Vercel, Executor, Sendblue, Upstash, Codex-account, secret, deployment, webhook, message, credential, approval, release, or production state.

The current Production preflight requires BUNDJIL_UPSTASH_REDIS_REST metadata while runtime reads UPSTASH_REDIS_REST or KV aliases; the Production runbook stops on this ambiguity and HGI-305 must resolve it before a positive Production packet.
HGI-304 still owns workflow and provider authority; HGI-305 still owns bounded critical-journey proof and improved blocked/preflight receipts.
The semantic and closeout commits are unsigned because the configured 1Password signing agent remains unavailable; content, tree, ancestry, push, and remote identity are independently verified.
nonClaims: No provider, environment value, ignored local file, workflow, deployment, credential, secret, webhook, message, approval resume, profile, database, release, or production system was read or changed.
A runbook, tool result, passing preflight, local check, fresh evaluator, or historical receipt grants no external-operation authority.
